questions.q: In the Ramayana, Agastya is best known as a great what?
questions.explanation: Agastya is one of the most revered rishis (sages) of Hindu tradition and a central figure of the Dandaka forest in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Agastya is famously called the sage of which direction?
questions.explanation: Agastya is celebrated as the great sage of the South, having made the southern lands habitable for hermits.

questions.q: In which forest does Rama visit Agastya during his exile?
questions.explanation: Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana visit Agastya's hermitage while wandering the Dandaka forest during their exile.

questions.q: What kind of divine weapon does Agastya gift to Rama?
questions.explanation: Agastya gives Rama a great bow, said to belong to Vishnu, along with other divine arms.

questions.q: Whose bow and quiver does Agastya say he is giving to Rama?
questions.explanation: Agastya presents Rama with the bow and inexhaustible quiver associated with the god Vishnu.

questions.q: Who accompanies Rama when he comes to Agastya's hermitage?
questions.explanation: Rama is accompanied by his wife Sita and his brother Lakshmana throughout the forest exile, including the visit to Agastya.

questions.q: Agastya receives Rama with what kind of welcome?
questions.explanation: Agastya warmly welcomes and honors Rama, recognizing him as a noble and worthy guest.

questions.q: Agastya's hermitage in the south provided safety for whom?
questions.explanation: By subduing demons in the region, Agastya made the south a refuge where ascetics and hermits could live in peace.

questions.q: What is the title given to Agastya on the card in this collection?
questions.explanation: In this collection, Agastya's card bears the subtitle 'The Sage of the South.'

questions.q: What does Agastya primarily advise Rama to do regarding the forest dwellers?
questions.explanation: Agastya encourages Rama to protect the sages and hermits of the forest from the rakshasas who torment them.

questions.q: According to tradition, Agastya was born from a pot or jar, giving him which epithet?
questions.explanation: Agastya is traditionally said to be born from a pitcher, earning epithets such as Kumbhayoni and Kumbhasambhava, 'pot-born.'

questions.q: Which two demon brothers did Agastya destroy in a famous legend, one cooked as food and the other digested?
questions.explanation: Agastya consumed the demon Vatapi, who could revive from a victim's stomach, and digested him, then incinerated his brother Ilvala.

questions.q: In legend, Agastya is said to have once swallowed which body of water?
questions.explanation: Agastya famously drank up the entire ocean to help the gods find demons hiding within it.

questions.q: Agastya is traditionally credited with humbling which mountain so it would stop growing?
questions.explanation: Agastya commanded the Vindhya mountains to bow low until his return, and as he settled in the south, they remained humbled.

questions.q: Who is the devoted wife of Agastya in tradition?
questions.explanation: Agastya's wife is Lopamudra, herself celebrated as a learned and virtuous woman in the tradition.

questions.q: Besides a great bow, what inexhaustible item does Agastya give Rama for the bow?
questions.explanation: Along with the bow, Agastya gives Rama a quiver said never to run empty of arrows.

questions.q: Agastya also gives Rama a sword and arrows associated with which other deity, alongside Vishnu's weapons?
questions.explanation: Among the divine arms Agastya bestows are weapons linked to Indra, including a celestial sword and arrows.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, on whose behalf had these divine weapons originally been kept before Agastya gave them to Rama?
options: For the purpose of destroying demons
To arm the army of Ravana
As a gift for Bharata's coronation
To be sold to forest tribes
questions.explanation: The weapons were divine arms meant for the destruction of demons, which Agastya entrusts to Rama for that very purpose.

questions.q: Agastya advises Rama to settle in which pleasant place after their meeting?
questions.explanation: Agastya recommends that Rama dwell at Panchavati on the Godavari, a beautiful and suitable spot for his hermitage.

questions.q: Agastya is counted among which famous group of primordial sages?
questions.explanation: Agastya is traditionally counted among the Saptarishis, the seven great sages of Hindu cosmology.

questions.q: Before reaching Agastya himself, Rama first visits the hermitage of whom, a relative of the sage?
questions.explanation: In the Aranya Kanda, Rama first comes to the ashram of Agastya's brother before proceeding to Agastya's own hermitage.

questions.q: What quality of Agastya is most emphasized when he speaks with Rama in the forest?
questions.explanation: Agastya is portrayed as a sage of immense tapas (austerity), spiritual power, and wisdom, which he shares with Rama.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what reason does Agastya give for the southern region having become safe for hermits?
options: He had subdued the rakshasas there
He had bought peace with gold
He had relocated all hermits north
questions.explanation: Agastya explains that he himself had curbed the rakshasas of the south, making the land safe for ascetics to dwell.

questions.q: Much later, in the Yuddha Kanda, Agastya teaches Rama which famous hymn before the final battle with Ravana?
questions.explanation: In the Yuddha Kanda, Agastya appears and teaches Rama the Aditya Hridayam, a hymn to the Sun, to give him strength against Ravana.

questions.q: The Aditya Hridayam taught by Agastya is a hymn in praise of which deity?
questions.explanation: The Aditya Hridayam is a devotional hymn to Surya, the Sun god, whom Rama worships at Agastya's instruction before defeating Ravana.

questions.q: In the legend of Vatapi and Ilvala, what trick did the demon Ilvala use against visiting brahmins?
options: He fed them his brother disguised as a ram, then called him back to life inside them
He turned them to stone with a glance
questions.explanation: Ilvala served his brother Vatapi, transformed into a ram, as food, then summoned him back to life inside the victim to tear them apart; Agastya digested Vatapi before this could happen.

questions.q: Why, in tradition, did Agastya order the Vindhya mountains to bow down?
options: The mountains had grown so tall they blocked the Sun's path
The mountains hid a demon army
The mountains had insulted the gods
The mountains had dried up the rivers
questions.explanation: The Vindhyas grew dangerously high, obstructing the Sun's course, so Agastya had them bow and stay low until his return from the south.

questions.q: According to the famous legend, why did Agastya drink the ocean dry?
options: So the gods could slay the Kalakeya demons hiding in it
To create new land for farming
To win a wager with Indra
questions.explanation: The demons known as Kalakeyas hid in the sea by day, so the gods asked Agastya to drink the ocean, exposing and allowing the demons to be slain.

questions.q: Which weapon, in addition to the bow of Vishnu, does Agastya specifically describe to Rama as forged by the divine architect?
options: A golden, jewel-set sword and arrows that never miss
A spear that controls the weather
questions.explanation: Agastya gives Rama divinely crafted arms, including a resplendent sword and unfailing arrows fashioned by celestial craft.

questions.q: In the broader tradition reflected in the Ramayana, Agastya is also revered as a pioneer of which cultural achievement in the south?
options: The spread of Vedic learning and language
The founding of sea trade with Rome
The building of the first stone temples
questions.explanation: Agastya is traditionally honored as the sage who carried Vedic learning and culture into the southern lands, civilizing the region.
